crops.name: Rice (Paddy)
crops.sowing_tip: Sow seeds in nursery, transplant at 3-4 leaf stage. Spacing: 20x15 cm.
crops.pest_alert: Watch for stem borer, brown plant hopper, and leaf folder.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.sowing_tip: Transplant at 25-30 days. Stake plants at 30 cm height. Mulch to reduce splash diseases.
crops.pest_alert: Watch for whitefly (transmits TYLCV), early blight, and fruit borer.

crops.name: Wheat
crops.sowing_tip: Sow at 20-22°C. Seed rate: 100 kg/ha. Irrigate at crown root initiation (21 days).
crops.pest_alert: Watch for yellow rust, loose smut, and aphids in cool humid conditions.

crops.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
crops.sowing_tip: Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium. Spacing: 30x10 cm. Keep field weed-free for first 45 days.
crops.pest_alert: Watch for leaf spot, tikka disease, and white grub.

crops.name: Banana
crops.sowing_tip: Plant suckers at 1.8x1.8 m spacing. Apply 200 g MOP per plant biannually.
crops.pest_alert: Watch for Panama wilt (Fusarium), banana bunchy top virus, and weevil borer.

crops.name: Cotton
crops.soil: Black cotton soil (Regur), deep clayey, pH 6.0-8.0
crops.sowing_tip: Spacing 90x45cm or 120x60cm depending on variety. Apply nitrogen in splits.
crops.pest_alert: Watch for Bollworms (Pink, Spotted), Whitefly, Thrips, Cotton Leaf Curl Virus.

crops.name: Maize (Corn)
crops.sowing_tip: Sow on ridges or raised beds. Spacing 60x20cm. Maintain proper soil moisture during tasseling and silking.
crops.pest_alert: Fall Armyworm (major threat), Stem Borer, Shoot Fly.

crops.name: Onion
crops.soil: Sandy loam to clay loam, well-drained, pH 6.0-7.0
crops.sowing_tip: Transplant 45-day nursery seedlings. Stop irrigation 2-3 weeks before harvest.

crops.name: Sugarcane
crops.soil: Deep rich loamy, clay loam, pH 6.5-7.5
crops.sowing_tip: Use two-budded setts. Plant in trenches at 90-120cm spacing. Earth up at 90-120 days.
crops.pest_alert: Early shoot borer, Internode borer, Red Rot.

crops.name: Chilli
crops.sowing_tip: Transplant 35-40 day seedlings. Spacing 60x45cm. Avoid water stagnation.
crops.pest_alert: Thrips, Mites, Fruit Borer, Leaf Curl Virus.

crops.name: Brinjal (Eggplant)
crops.soil: Well-drained sandy loam or clay loam, pH 5.5-6.8
crops.sowing_tip: Transplant 4-5 week old seedlings at 60x60cm spacing.
crops.pest_alert: Shoot and Fruit Borer (major pest), Epilachna beetle, Phomopsis blight.

crops.name: Okra (Bhindi)
crops.soil: Sandy loam to clay loam, pH 6.0-6.8
crops.pest_alert: Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus (transmitted by Whitefly), Fruit Borer.

crops.name: Turmeric
crops.soil: Well-drained sandy or clay loam, rich in organic matter, pH 4.5-7.5
crops.sowing_tip: Plant rhizomes at 30x15cm on ridges. Apply liberal organic manure.
crops.pest_alert: Shoot borer, Rhizome rot, Leaf blotch.

crops.name: Millets (Bajra/Jowar)
crops.soil: Sandy to loam, tolerates poor soils, pH 5.5-7.5
crops.sowing_tip: Direct sowing in lines at 45x15cm. Needs very little water.
crops.pest_alert: Shoot fly, Stem borer, Downy mildew.

crops.name: Mango
crops.npk: N:1000g, P:500g, K:1000g per mature tree/year
crops.sowing_tip: Plant grafts at 10x10m spacing (or 5x5m for high density). Prune regularly.
crops.pest_alert: Mango hopper, Mealybug, Fruit fly, Powdery mildew.

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.symptoms: Dead heart at vegetative stage, white ear at booting stage, frass inside stem.
pests.organic_control: Release Trichogramma japonicum (50,000/ha) at egg-laying stage. Use pheromone traps.
pests.chemical_control: Cartap Hydrochloride 4G @ 10 kg/acre OR Chlorpyrifos 20EC @ 2.5 ml/litre.
pests.cultural_control: Avoid close planting. Remove and burn stubble after harvest.

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.symptoms: Yellowing, leaf curl, sooty mold on leaves. Vectors TYLCV in tomato.
pests.organic_control: Yellow sticky traps (10/acre). Spray Neem oil @ 3 ml/litre. Encourage natural enemies.
pests.cultural_control: Use reflective silver mulch. Destroy volunteer plants near field.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.symptoms: Curled, distorted leaves. Sticky honeydew deposits. Ant activity on plants.
pests.organic_control: Spray Neem seed kernel extract 5% OR Beauveria bassiana @ 5 ml/litre.
pests.cultural_control: Avoid late sowing. Conserve lady bird beetles (Coccinellids) as natural enemies.

pests.name: Fall Armyworm (FAW)
pests.symptoms: Large ragged holes in leaves, frass in the whorl.
pests.organic_control: Release Trichogramma pretiosum. Neem oil spray 5 ml/litre. Sand+Ash in whorls.
pests.chemical_control: Spinetoram 11.7 SC @ 0.5 ml/litre or Emamectin benzoate 5 SG @ 0.4 g/litre in whorl.
pests.cultural_control: Deep summer ploughing. Intercropping with legumes (push-pull strategy).

pests.name: Brown Plant Hopper (BPH)
pests.symptoms: Hopper burn (drying of plants in patches). Nymphs and adults at base of tillers.
pests.organic_control: Avoid excessive urea. Alternate wetting and drying. Neem formulations.
pests.chemical_control: Pymetrozine 50 WG @ 0.6 g/litre or Dinotefuran 20 SG @ 0.4 g/litre directed at base.
pests.cultural_control: Provide 30cm alleyways every 2-3 meters. Grow resistant varieties.

pests.name: Powdery Mildew
pests.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves, stems, and fruits.
pests.chemical_control: Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 1 ml/litre or Myclobutanil 10 WP @ 1 g/litre.

government_schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
government_schemes.benefit: Loans up to ₹3 lakh at 4% interest (with prompt repayment)

government_schemes.name: PM KUSUM
government_schemes.benefit: Subsidy (up to 60%) to set up solar pumps

government_schemes.name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
government_schemes.benefit: Pan-India electronic trading portal for better prices

fertilizers.name: Urea (46-0-0)
fertilizers.application: Split doses — 1/3 basal, 1/3 tillering, 1/3 panicle initiation. Neem-coated preferred.

fertilizers.name: DAP (18-46-0)
fertilizers.application: Apply before sowing. Incorporate into soil.

fertilizers.name: Muriate of Potash (MOP)
fertilizers.application: Apply as basal or split depending on soil K status. Improves grain quality and drought tolerance.

fertilizers.name: Single Super Phosphate (SSP)
fertilizers.application: Good for oilseeds (groundnut, mustard) due to sulphur content. Use as basal dose.

irrigation_methods.name: Drip Irrigation
irrigation_methods.benefits: Minimizes weed growth, reduces disease risk, enables fertigation (applying fertilizer through water), high yield.

irrigation_methods.name: Sprinkler Irrigation
irrigation_methods.benefits: Good for sandy soils and undulating land, creates micro-climate.

irrigation_methods.name: Flood Irrigation
irrigation_methods.benefits: Cheap setup. Necessary for traditional paddy to suppress weeds.

soil_health.name: Black Soil (Regur)
soil_health.characteristics: High clay content, poor drainage, swells when wet, cracks when dry. Rich in lime, iron, magnesia.

soil_health.name: Red & Laterite Soil
soil_health.characteristics: Sandy to loam, good drainage, poor in Nitrogen/Phosphorus/Humus. Rich in Iron (red color).

soil_health.name: Alluvial Soil
soil_health.characteristics: Highly fertile, loam to clay loam, rich in potash but poor in phosphorus.
